skill.name: theme-factory
skill.description: Applies curated color and font themes to presentations, documents, reports, and HTML artifacts, with ten built-in styles and custom theme generation.
skill.summary: Apply consistent color and font themes to visual artifacts.
skill.author: Cam10001110101

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported high and medium patterns, but review found false positives in Apache License prose, Markdown inline code spans, color hex values, and theme wording. No evidence found of command execution, network behavior, prompt injection, credential access, or data exfiltration in the reviewed skill files.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Network URL Findings in License Text
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are standard Apache License reference links. They are documentation text and are not used by executable code or instructions that make network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are the canonical Apache License URL and license-copy URL. There is no surrounding code or instruction that fetches these addresses.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Findings in Plain Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts occur in license prose and theme descriptions. The reviewed context contains no hashing, encryption, signing, password handling, or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged files are Markdown or license text and contain no executable cryptographic operations. The repeated matches are lexical collisions with ordinary prose and theme names.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Markdown Backtick Command Findings
low_findings.description: The reported backticks are Markdown formatting around filenames and color hex values. They do not define Ruby, shell, or any other executable command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited theme line is a Markdown bullet with an inline hex color code. The SKILL.md citations are inline filenames, not executable code.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Command-and-Control Keyword Finding
low_findings.description: The C2 keyword alert is from a visual theme color name in a palette. It does not reference servers, beacons, persistence, callbacks, or remote control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line describes a lavender accent color for a presentation theme. No network endpoint or operational malware context is present.

content.value_statement: Unstyled slides, reports, and pages can look inconsistent and slow down delivery. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code apply curated color and font systems quickly.
actual_capabilities: Provides ten named themes with color palettes and font pairings.
Guides the assistant to show a theme showcase PDF before selection.
Applies selected colors and fonts consistently across an artifact.
Supports presentations, documents, reports, and HTML landing pages.
Can generate a custom theme from user-provided style direction.
Includes guidance to maintain contrast, readability, and visual identity.
limitations: It does not include scripts or automated file conversion tools.
Artifact editing depends on the host AI tool and available document access.
Specified fonts may need substitution if they are unavailable locally.
It does not perform formal accessibility or brand compliance validation.

use_cases.title: Refresh a Client Deck
use_cases.description: Apply a professional palette and typography system to make a presentation consistent before delivery.

use_cases.title: Style a Product Report
use_cases.description: Use a theme to align charts, section headers, and narrative pages with a clear visual direction.

use_cases.title: Create a Custom Campaign Look
use_cases.description: Generate a new theme from campaign goals, then apply it across slides or HTML content.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Theme
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner selection from the built-in showcase
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the available theme options for this artifact, then ask which one I want to apply.

prompt_templates.title: Apply a Selected Theme
prompt_templates.scenario: Use one named theme on an existing artifact
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the Ocean Depths theme to this presentation. Keep the existing content and improve consistency, contrast, and typography.

prompt_templates.title: Match an Audience
prompt_templates.scenario: Select a theme based on context
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend the best built-in theme for an executive product strategy deck, explain the choice briefly, then apply it after confirmation.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Custom System
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a custom theme for a premium health analytics report. Include a name, color palette, font pairings, use guidance, and apply it after review.

output_examples.input: Apply a calm professional theme to a finance deck.
output: The artifact uses deep navy for structure, teal for emphasis, and light seafoam accents.
Headers and body text are standardized for a polished consulting style.

output_examples.input: Create a custom theme for a boutique interior design proposal.
output: The palette uses warm neutrals, muted clay, deep charcoal, and a soft ivory base.
The result supports elegant layouts, restrained highlights, and readable long-form sections.

output_examples.input: Make this technology launch page feel sharper and more modern.
output: Applied a high-contrast technology theme with electric accents.
Primary calls to action, headings, dividers, and supporting sections now share one visual system.

best_practices: Review the theme showcase before applying a theme to important work.
Confirm the selected theme with the user before changing the artifact.
Check contrast and readability after applying colors and fonts.
anti_patterns: Do not mix several built-in themes in one artifact without a clear reason.
Do not replace content structure when the user only asked for styling.
Do not assume a custom theme is final before the user reviews it.

faq.question: What does Theme Factory do?
faq.answer: It helps apply consistent color palettes and font pairings to slides, documents, reports, and HTML artifacts.

faq.question: How many built-in themes are included?
faq.answer: The skill includes ten built-in themes, each with named colors, hex values, typography, and suggested use cases.

faq.question: Can it create a new theme?
faq.answer: Yes. It can generate a custom theme from user-provided style goals and then ask for review.

faq.question: Does it run scripts or commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed files contain Markdown instructions, theme descriptions, a license file, and a PDF showcase.

faq.question: Can it edit every artifact type automatically?
faq.answer: No. The actual edit depends on the host tool, file access, and supported document formats.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Static alerts were reviewed as false positives, and no malicious behavior was found in the reviewed files.
